summary: Machine policy for cellular message cloud sync. Microsoft documents the same device policy path, value name, default, and numeric meanings that the app writes.

setting.name: Message Service Cloud Sync
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can back up and restore cellular text messages through Microsoft's cloud services. This policy decides whether that cloud sync is allowed on the device.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it to keep text messages off Microsoft's cloud services or to reduce cloud-linked personal data flows from the device.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off reduces cloud backup of messages, but it also removes the convenience of Microsoft's message backup and restore behavior.
setting.professional_notes: The Messaging Policy CSP documents AllowMessageSync directly. messaging.admx maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Messaging\AllowMessageSync with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the message cloud-sync policy.

allowed_values.label: Message cloud sync disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Backup and restore of cellular text messages to Microsoft's cloud services is blocked by policy.

allowed_values.label: Message cloud sync allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The device is allowed to use Microsoft's cloud backup and restore path for cellular messages.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes AllowMessageSync = 0, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 1709 and newer systems with the Messaging policy available

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This is the policy baseline and does not by itself say whether the device has cellular messaging features in use.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that want Microsoft's backup and restore flow for messages
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal policy baseline for message cloud sync.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want SMS and MMS data kept out of Microsoft's cloud backup path.
tradeoffs: Cloud backup and restore of messages can remain available.
No policy-based restriction is enforced for this data flow.

recommended_profiles.label: Privacy focused
avoid_for: Users who rely on Microsoft's message backup and restore behavior

states.rationale: Disable Microsoft's cloud backup and restore path for cellular messages.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want cellular text messages kept out of Microsoft's cloud sync flow.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want message backup and restore convenience through Microsoft's services.
tradeoffs: Cloud sync of cellular text messages is disabled.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: Messaging / AllowMessageSync
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the policy that controls backup and restore of cellular text messages to Microsoft's cloud services.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft messaging.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows AllowMessageSync with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the Messaging policy key, and the local ADML help text describes backup and restore of cellular text messages to Microsoft's cloud services.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes AllowMessageSync = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - AllowMessageSync policy
evidence.summary: Messaging policy metadata shows AllowMessageSync under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Messaging. The mirrored table and ExplainText say 0 forbids SMS/MMS cloud sync and user changes, while 1 allows sync and user control. The policy controls cloud backup and restore of cellular text messages.

decision.why: Microsoft documents AllowMessageSync directly in the Messaging Policy CSP and maps it to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Messaging\AllowMessageSync with 0 as blocked and 1 as the default allowed state. The app writes that exact machine policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Default Value 1; Allowed values: 0 Message sync isn't allowed and can't be changed by the user. 1 (Default) Message sync is allowed. The user can change this setting. Group policy mapping: Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Messaging; Registry Value Name AllowMessageSync.
validation_proof.notes: The Messaging Policy CSP page explicitly names the exact machine policy path and value name the app writes and defines the 0/1 semantics. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-allowmessagesync-admx.
